_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the diagnostic EGD with duodenal biopsy this patient was referred for.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) with duodenal biopsy
procedure.indication: Serology-suspected celiac disease (tTG-IgA >10x ULN, IgA-sufficient) with iron deficiency; confirmatory duodenal biopsy on a gluten-containing diet
procedure.sedation: Moderate sedation (midazolam 2 mg, fentanyl 75 mcg); topical pharyngeal anesthesia
procedure.prep_quality: N/A (upper endoscopy); patient NPO x 8 hours, adequate fasting
procedure.extent_reached: Second/third portion of duodenum (post-bulbar); esophagus, stomach, and duodenum fully examined

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy with biopsy. INDICATION: markedly elevated tTG-IgA (>10x ULN, IgA-sufficient) with iron deficiency; confirmatory duodenal biopsy. Patient confirmed to have continued a gluten-containing diet up to the procedure. SEDATION: moderate. FINDINGS: The endoscope was passed under direct vision. ESOPHAGUS: normal mucosa, no esophagitis, normal Z-line, no varices. STOMACH: normal gastric mucosa on forward and retroflexed views; no ulcer or mass; antrum normal. DUODENUM: the duodenal bulb and second/third portions were examined. Mucosa showed scalloping of the folds, mosaic/fissured pattern, and reduced fold height in the distal duodenum, findings suggestive of villous atrophy consistent with celiac disease. No ulcer, mass, or active bleeding. Biopsies obtained: four specimens from the second/third portion of the duodenum (distal) and two from the duodenal bulb, submitted separately per Marsh-protocol sampling. IMPRESSION: Endoscopic findings suggestive of villous atrophy (scalloping, mosaic pattern) consistent with celiac disease; duodenal biopsies obtained for confirmation. No bleeding source or mass to explain iron deficiency identified on upper exam. Pathology pending.

findings.finding: Duodenal mucosal scalloping, mosaic/fissured pattern, reduced fold height suggestive of villous atrophy
Normal esophageal mucosa, no esophagitis or varices
Normal gastric mucosa, no ulcer or mass on forward and retroflexed views

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Tolerated procedure well, no immediate complications. Post-endoscopy and sedation precautions reviewed.

followup.recommendation: Review duodenal biopsy pathology (Marsh grade); call patient with result to confirm or exclude celiac disease and update problem list from suspected to confirmed/excluded
If celiac confirmed, refer to registered dietitian for gluten-free diet counseling and initiate gluten-free diet
Reassess iron studies (Hgb, ferritin) after diagnosis; continue multivitamin with iron; iron deficiency likely secondary to malabsorption if celiac confirmed
Update problem list: celiac disease currently serology-suspected pending histology; add OTC omega-3 (fish oil) and family history (sister with biopsy-confirmed celiac) to record

planted_followup_items.description: Duodenal biopsy pathology pending (Marsh grade) — the confirmatory test for celiac disease; result must be communicated to the patient and drive the diagnosis
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with pathology result; confirm or exclude celiac; update problem list from suspected/provisional to final

planted_followup_items.description: Dietitian referral for gluten-free diet counseling contingent on positive biopsy
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place dietitian referral once celiac confirmed; initiate gluten-free diet

planted_followup_items.description: Post-diagnosis follow-up: repeat tTG-IgA and iron studies to assess response to gluten-free diet
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule follow-up labs (tTG-IgA, Hgb, ferritin) at ~3 months and clinic visit

planted_followup_items.description: Iron deficiency (Hgb 12.9, ferritin 18) — no bleeding source or mass on upper endoscopy; attribute to malabsorption if celiac confirmed, otherwise reassess and consider further workup
planted_followup_items.expected_action: recheck iron studies after treatment; if not resolving or celiac excluded, pursue additional GI evaluation

planted_followup_items.description: OTC omega-3 (fish oil), misreported by patient as a 'blood thinner' — not a true anticoagulant, no periprocedural hold required; ensure it is added to the medication list
planted_followup_items.expected_action: reconcile medication list to include OTC fish oil

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels scratchy and I'm kind of burpy and bloated since the scope. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and air insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating are expected after upper endoscopy and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will I hear back about the biopsy results? And should I keep taking my fish oil blood thinner or stop it now?
contacts.clinical_signal: Question about pending duodenal pathology plus 'blood thinner' confusion (OTC fish oil)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: duodenal pathology pending (~day 4-5), team will call with the Marsh grade and confirm or exclude celiac. Clarify the fish oil is an OTC omega-3 supplement, not a true blood thinner — no need to stop or hold it; it has been added to the medication list.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item and closes the med-confusion loop (trap: 'blood thinner' is benign fish oil).

contacts.verbatim: Since yesterday I've had a fever of about 101.5, a cough with some junk coming up, and I get short of breath just walking to the kitchen. It started the day after the scope.
contacts.clinical_signal: Fever, productive cough, and dyspnea 2 days post-sedation EGD — concern for aspiration pneumonitis/pneumonia from procedural sedation
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day evaluation (urgent clinic or ED): assess oxygen saturation, chest exam, and chest imaging for post-sedation aspiration; treat as aspiration pneumonia if confirmed. Do not manage as benign post-endoscopy symptoms.
contacts.rationale: New fever plus productive cough and exertional dyspnea shortly after sedated upper endoscopy is a genuine post-procedure complication (aspiration) requiring prompt in-person evaluation, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: I noticed a little pink tinge when I spit the first day or two after the biopsies. It's gone now — was that a problem?
contacts.clinical_signal: Trace transient blood-tinged saliva after mucosal duodenal biopsies, self-resolved
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; minor transient pink-tinged saliva after superficial mucosal biopsies is expected and self-limited. No action needed given it has resolved and there is no ongoing bleeding, black stools, or lightheadedness.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign trap: minor self-resolved spotting after mucosal biopsy is not a bleeding complication and correctly resolves to reassurance.
